source_jobs.title: Head of Product
source_jobs.description:
Wildfires are a $20 trillion crisis. Earth Force is building the technology that dramatically increases the pace and scale of fuels reduction and forest thinning—creating resilient forests and protecting people and communities from the devastating effects of wildfire.

We develop integrated computer vision systems—hardware through UI—deployed on forestry machines operating in remote forest environments. Our platform measures trees in real time, automates production reporting, and enables precision silviculture at a scale the industry has never seen. We work primarily with the U.S. Forest Service and their implementation partners under multi-year program agreements.

We’re commercially deployed and approaching scale. Our next season of growth will deliver on the capabilities our tech has developed, broadening the trust we've built with our partners to serve those working to protect land from catastrophic wildfire.

________________________________________________________________________

Earth Force is seeking a Head of Product to lead the Product function for the Guide business unit.

This role will own product leadership, roadmap execution, and customer-domain product strategy for Guide — translating company-level product direction into actionable priorities that support field deployments, customer outcomes, and business growth.

The Head of Product will partner closely with Earth Force’s Chief Product Officer, who leads centralized product strategy and platform direction across the company. Within that framework, this role is responsible for ensuring the Guide roadmap, customer requirements, and deployment priorities are effectively defined, integrated, and executed.

This role sits at the center of a highly cross-functional ecosystem spanning hardware, computer vision, field operations, and customer workflows. Success requires strong product judgment, systems thinking, and the ability to create alignment across technical, operational, and commercial stakeholders operating in complex real-world environments.

This is also a foundational organizational leadership role. The Head of Product will help shape how the Guide product organization operates as Earth Force scales from early deployments toward production-ready systems — including building product management practices, establishing planning rhythms, and hiring domain-focused Product Managers over time.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the Guide product roadmap, translating company strategy into prioritized product initiatives, delivery plans, and execution milestones.

 - Partner cross-functionally with Engineering, Architecture, Operations, Commercial, and Product leadership to align priorities, requirements, tradeoffs, and releases.

 - Incorporate customer, operator, and field feedback into product decisions, ensuring solutions address real-world workflows and deployment challenges.

 - Balance short-term deployment needs with long-term platform, product, and business objectives to drive sustainable growth.

 - Build and scale the Guide Product function, including team development, product management processes, documentation standards, and prioritization frameworks.

 - Drive deployment readiness and production scalability by defining success criteria and ensuring products support reliable field operations and commercial adoption.


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 6+ years of product management experience, including leadership responsibility.

 - Experience leading products that combine hardware and software systems.

 - Strong systems thinker comfortable operating across technical, operational, and customer domains simultaneously.

 - Proven ability to partner effectively with highly technical engineering and architecture teams.

 - Comfortable navigating ambiguity and building structure in fast-moving environments.

 - Strong product judgment and prioritization instincts.

 - Excellent communicator capable of aligning diverse stakeholders around difficult tradeoffs.

 -

source_jobs.title: Director of Program Success
source_jobs.description:
Wildfires are a $20 trillion crisis. Earth Force is building the technology that dramatically increases the pace and scale of fuels reduction and forest thinning—creating resilient forests and protecting people and communities from the devastating effects of wildfire.

We develop integrated computer vision systems—hardware through UI—deployed on forestry machines operating in remote forest environments. Our platform measures trees in real time, automates production reporting, and enables precision silviculture at a scale the industry has never seen. We work primarily with the U.S. Forest Service and their implementation partners under multi-year program agreements.

We’re commercially deployed and approaching scale. Our next season of growth will deliver on the capabilities our tech has developed, broadening the trust we've built with our partners to serve those working to protect land from catastrophic wildfire.

________________________________________________________________________

We are seeking a Director of Program Success to own the delivery lifecycle for our customer programs—from pre-contract scoping through steady-state operations and expansion.

This is not a traditional customer success role. Our engagements are multi-year contracted programs with long term deliverables. We serve a complex ecosystem of customers and users from the U.S. Forest Service, to forestry project administrators, to machine operators in the woods. The technology is hardware deployed on heavy equipment in remote terrain. Success is measured by delivery against long-term commitments, and consistent improvement in our customer satisfaction over time

You’ll sit at the convergence of three functions that must work together for our customers to succeed: Operations (field delivery and hardware), Product and Engineering (system capability and reliability), and our Partnerships team (commercial commitments and account growth). You’ll be the person who ensures that what we promise is grounded in what we can deliver—and that what we deliver meets the customer’s definition of program success.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own end-to-end delivery of customer programs, including onboarding, commissioning, operational milestones, SLA management, and long-term program success.

 - Proactively identify and resolve delivery risks across Operations, Product, and Engineering before they impact customers.

 - Partner with commercial and partnerships teams to ensure customer commitments, timelines, and SLAs align with operational and technical capabilities.

 - Serve as the primary program-level contact for customers and partners, leading engagement cadences, reporting, QBRs, and issue resolution.

 - Build and scale program delivery infrastructure, including playbooks, escalation protocols, reporting frameworks, and commissioning processes.

 - Translate customer contracts into measurable operational plans while coordinating cross-functional execution across field, product, and engineering teams.

 - Help establish and scale Earth Force’s long-term program management function, processes, and operating model as the company grows.


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 7+ years managing complex, multi-stakeholder programs with contractual commitments and SLAs.

 - Strong program delivery ownership and accountability for execution against customer commitments.

 - Experience working directly in the field and resolving operational issues onsite.

 - Comfortable operating in remote, physically demanding environments and around heavy equipment.

 - Proven ability to coordinate across Operations, Product, Engineering, and customer stakeholders.

 - Ability to translate field realities into executive-level communication and operational plans.

 - Experience building or shaping program management, delivery, or customer engagement functions from the ground up.

 - Experience working with federal agencies, public-sector organizations, or highly regulated industries.


PREFERRED Q

source_jobs.title: Hardware Manufacturing Lead
source_jobs.description:
Wildfires are a $20 trillion crisis. Earth Force is building the technology that dramatically increases the pace and scale of fuels reduction and forest thinning—creating resilient forests and protecting people and communities from the devastating effects of wildfire.

We develop integrated computer vision systems—hardware through UI—deployed on forestry machines operating in remote forest environments. Our platform measures trees in real time, automates production reporting, and enables precision silviculture at a scale the industry has never seen. We work primarily with the U.S. Forest Service and their implementation partners under multi-year program agreements.

We’re commercially deployed and approaching scale. Our next season of growth will deliver on the capabilities our tech has developed, broadening the trust we've built with our partners to serve those working to protect land from catastrophic wildfire.

________________________________________________________________________

We are seeking a Hardware Manufacturing Lead to join Earth Force and take ownership of how our hardware systems are built as we transition from prototype deployments to in-house manufacturing and scaled production.

This role sits between engineering, operations, and the field. You will translate prototype systems into repeatable, manufacturable assemblies and help build the internal manufacturing capability required to support our growth.

This is a hands-on role in a small team. You will be expected to understand our full hardware ecosystem and help us build reliable systems that can be produced, deployed, serviced, and improved over time. In the near term, this role will focus on electrical systems, manufacturing processes, documentation, and test procedures. Over time, this role is expected to grow into a leadership position responsible for manufacturing and production at Earth Force.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead the transition from prototype and contract manufacturing to scalable in-house hardware production and assembly.

 - Develop and document manufacturing processes, assembly instructions, wiring diagrams, test procedures, and production workflows.

 - Own electrical systems integration, troubleshooting, testing, and cross-functional coordination across hardware, software, operations, and field teams.

 - Drive design-for-manufacturing (DFM) improvements to increase reliability, manufacturability, serviceability, and cost efficiency.

 - Build and maintain production infrastructure, including supply chain coordination, tooling, test equipment, Device Master Records (DMRs), and revision control processes.


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - In-person in Portland, Oregon.

 - Degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related field, or equivalent practical experience.

 - Experience building and troubleshooting real-world electrical and mechanical systems.

 - Experience moving a product from prototype to low-volume production.

 - Experience creating documentation such as assembly instructions, wiring diagrams, and test procedures.

 - Hands-on skills including wiring, soldering, harness building, mechanical assembly, and system debugging.

 - Experience working with suppliers, contract manufacturers, or production vendors.

 - Ability to work in a small team and take ownership of large portions of a system.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience with ruggedized electronics or outdoor equipment.

 - Experience with vehicle power systems (12V/24V), power conditioning, and ignition systems.

 - Experience with sensors, cameras, LiDAR, GPS, or communications systems.

 - Experience building test fixtures and production test processes.

 - Experience with CAD for basic mechanical integration and packaging.

 - Experience in industries such as forestry, agriculture, construction, mining, marine, robotics, or defense hardware.

notes: Computer vision and hardware development for forestry industry
